courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Fundamentals of Public Speaking
courses.description: Introduction to public speaking and oral communication with applied practice; emphasis on logical reasoning, critical listening, and contexts relevant to Asian American communities.

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Freshman Speech Communication
courses.description: Introduction to human communication with intensive practice in public speaking, logical reasoning, and critical listening; African/Black studies contexts.

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Fundamentals of Public Speaking
courses.description: Fundamentals of public speaking and verbal communication; practice in organization, delivery, and critical listening with Central American studies topics.

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Freshman Speech Communication
courses.description: Principles and practice of effective verbal communication and public speaking; focus on reasoning, critical listening, and delivery in Chicana and Chicano Studies contexts.

courses.subject: Communication Studies
courses.title: Fundamentals of Public Speaking
courses.description: Introduction to principles of effective public speaking with intensive practice in speech development, delivery, and analysis.

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Fundamentals of QS Public Speaking
courses.description: Public speaking with emphasis on issues and contexts significant to LGBTQIA+ communities; practice in reasoning, preparation, and delivery.

courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing focusing on logical thought, clear expression, organization, diction, syntax, and grammar; Asian American Studies contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing emphasizing logical thought, clear expression, organization, diction, syntax, and grammar; Africana Studies contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing with practice in organization, diction, syntax, grammar, and effective use of evidence; Central American Studies topics.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing focusing on logical reasoning, organization, diction, syntax, and grammar; Chicana/o Studies contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: English
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing with a focus on content and form; development of organizational strategies and effective use of evidence; instruction in diction, syntax, and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Linguistics
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing for international/non-native speakers; focus on logic, clear expression, organization, and conventions of English prose.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing with emphasis on logical thought, organization, diction, syntax, and grammar; topics framed through LGBTQIA+ contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 113A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113A).

courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing with focus on logical thought, clear expression, organizational strategies, and evidence use; instruction in diction, syntax and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing with focus on content and form; development of organizational strategies and appropriate gathering and use of evidence; diction, syntax and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing on both content and form; practice in organizational strategies and effective use of evidence; instruction in diction, syntax and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing focused on logical thought, clear expression, organizational strategies, and gathering/using evidence; instruction in diction, syntax and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: English
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing emphasizing logical thought and clear expression; development of organizational strategies and appropriate use of evidence; diction, syntax, grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing A
courses.description: Expository prose writing focusing on content and form; development of organizational strategies and effective evidence use; instruction in diction, syntax and grammar.

restrictions.text: Placement in supported GE A2 Written Communication. Credit for only one of the cross-listed 114A courses (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114A).

courses.subject: University
courses.title: Supplemental Instruction in Writing
courses.description: Mandatory supplemental instruction for 113B writing courses; peer-led sessions supporting university writing. Up to 1 unit of non-baccalaureate credit per term.

restrictions.text: Non-baccalaureate credit; must be taken concurrently with a 113B course.

courses.subject: University
courses.title: Supplemental Instruction in Writing
courses.description: Mandatory supplemental instruction for 113A writing courses; peer-led sessions supporting university writing. Provides up to 1 additional unit of non-baccalaureate credit per term.

restrictions.text: Non-baccalaureate credit; must be taken concurrently with a 113A course.

courses.subject: English
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with emphasis on logical thought, clear expression, organization strategies, and effective use of evidence.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one cross-listed 113B course (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113B).

courses.subject: English
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing; practice in diction, syntax, grammar, and elements of prose style with continued development of academic writing.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one cross-listed 114B course (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 114B).

courses.subject: English
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository prose writing focusing on logical thought and clear expression; instruction in organization, grammar, and elements of prose style.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement). Credit for only one cross-listed 115 course (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/QS 115).

courses.subject: Linguistics
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing emphasizing organization, diction, syntax, grammar, and effective use of evidence.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one cross-listed 113B course (AAS/AFRS/CAS/CHS/ENGL/LING/QS 113B).

courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with Asian American Studies contexts; emphasis on logical reasoning, organization, and critical use of sources.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one cross-listed 113B course.

courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing; practice in style and organization with Asian American Studies topics.

courses.subject: Asian American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository writing with Asian American Studies applications; development of clear expression and effective organization.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement).

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with emphases on logic, organization, and clear expression in Africana Studies contexts.

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with continued development of style and organization in Africana Studies topics.

courses.subject: Africana Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository writing focusing on clarity, organization, and evidence use in Africana Studies contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement).

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with Central American Studies topics; practice in organization and critical reasoning.

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing; emphasis on diction, syntax, grammar, and prose style.

courses.subject: Central American Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository writing with attention to organization and effective argumentation.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement).

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing focused on logical reasoning, organization, and delivery in Chicana/o Studies contexts.

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing; continued development of style, grammar, and organization in Chicana/o Studies.

courses.subject: Chicana and Chicano Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository writing emphasizing clear expression and effective organization in Chicana/o Studies contexts.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement).

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing with emphasis on reasoning, organization, and critical listening in LGBTQIA+ contexts.

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing B
courses.description: Expository prose writing; continued development of diction, syntax, grammar, and prose style in LGBTQIA+ contexts.

courses.subject: Queer Studies
courses.title: Approaches to University Writing
courses.description: Expository writing with practice in reasoning, preparation, and delivery from queer perspectives.

restrictions.text: Placement at GE-level writing (Multiple Measures Placement).

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Introduction to Environmental Science
courses.description: How human activities affect the environment; ecology, atmospheric and hydrologic science, climate change, energy use, conservation and sustainable agriculture. May satisfy B3 lab by completing a related lab when offered.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: General Physics I
courses.description: Introductory mechanics, heat and sound for B1 Physical Science.

restrictions.text: Background in MATH 105 or MATH 106 or sufficient preparation for entry into MATH 150A or MATH 255A. B3 lab may be satisfied by PHYS 100AL.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: General Physics II
courses.description: Continuation of PHYS 100A. Electricity and magnetism, light and modern physics.

restrictions.text: B3 lab may be satisfied by PHYS 100BL.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Mechanics
courses.description: Dynamics and statics of particles and rigid bodies; vibrations and fluid mechanics.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite or preparatory: MATH 150B or MATH 255B. B3 lab may be satisfied by PHYS 220AL.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Electricity and Magnetism
courses.description: Electric and magnetic fields, circuits, electromagnetic induction.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite or preparatory: MATH 250. B3 lab may be satisfied by PHYS 220BL.

courses.subject: Science
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change
courses.description: Basic science behind climate predictions; causes and impacts of climate change; mitigation and economic barriers.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one course from SCI 111, GEOG 111, or SUST 111.

courses.subject: Sustainability
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change
courses.description: Science of climate change, projections, mitigation and societal context; sustainability focus.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one course from SUST 111, GEOG 111, or SCI 111.

courses.subject: Astronomy
courses.title: Elementary Astronomy
courses.description: History of astronomy, laws governing the universe, stars and galaxies, origins and evolution; Big Bang overview.

restrictions.text: Students should enroll in ASTR 152 only if meeting the B3 lab in Life Science. No credit with ASTR 154.

courses.subject: Astronomy
courses.title: Elementary Astronomy
courses.description: Astronomy survey: history, laws, properties of stars/galaxies, origin and evolution, Big Bang.

restrictions.text: No credit with ASTR 152.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Principles of Chemistry
courses.description: Systematic, semi-empirical introduction to the submicroscopic world; atomic/molecular structure, bonding, formation and reactivity; applications to life and environmental sciences.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite: GE-level Math placement or exemption. Engineering/biology/physical science majors should consult advisors. B3 lab may be satisfied by CHEM 100L.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: General Chemistry I
courses.description: Principles and theories of chemistry with emphasis on calculations; kinetic molecular theory, atomic structure, periodicity, solutions, oxidation-reduction.

restrictions.text: Satisfactory score on Chemistry Placement Test (CPT) or grade of C or higher in CHEM 100 at CSUN only. All three components (CHEM 101, 101D, 101L) must be completed before CHEM 333/333D/333L or CHEM 321/L.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: General Chemistry II
courses.description: Continuation of CHEM 101: kinetics, gas phase and solution equilibria, electrochemistry, thermodynamics, and radio/nuclear chemistry.

restrictions.text: Minimum grade of C− in CHEM 101. B3 lab may be satisfied by CHEM 102L.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Climate Change
courses.description: Scientific basis of climate change; greenhouse gases, impacts, projections, mitigation, and economics.

restrictions.text: No credit in addition to GEOG 111, SUST 111, or SCI 111.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geology of Planet Earth
courses.description: Earth materials and processes; tectonics, hazards, glacial/river/coastal processes; resources and pollution; field trips.

restrictions.text: No credit for both GEOL 100 and GEOL 101. B3 lab may be satisfied by GEOL 102.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geology Goes Hollywood
courses.description: Online lecture exploring Earth science issues in film and TV and how media influences perceptions.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: The Fossil Record of Ancient Life on Earth
courses.description: Dynamic history of Earth's evolution and life over geologic time; dinosaurs to humans; geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, atmosphere.

restrictions.text: No credit for both GEOL 110 and GEOL 113. Enroll in GEOL 110 only if taking a different course than GEOL 112 to meet B3 lab.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Earth and Life Through Time
courses.description: Evolution, environmental change, and Earth history over 4.6 billion years using the fossil record.

restrictions.text: No credit for both GEOL 113 and GEOL 110.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geological Disasters
courses.description: Lecture/lab combo on geologic hazards: earthquakes, volcanoes, landslides, flooding; risk evaluation and mitigation.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: The World Ocean
courses.description: Introduction to ocean basins, environments, and resources; biological, chemical, geological, and physical oceanography.

restrictions.text: B3 lab may be satisfied by GEOL 123.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Introductory Chemistry I
courses.description: Fundamentals of inorganic chemistry: atomic structure, molecules, periodic table, states of matter, stoichiometry and basic algebraic operations.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite: GE-level Math placement or exemption. Not open to engineering, biology, or physical science majors. B3 lab may be satisfied by CHEM 103L.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Introductory Chemistry II
courses.description: Continuation of CHEM 103; properties of solutions, equilibria, acids/bases, and chemistry of simple organic compounds and common elements.

restrictions.text: Not open to engineering, biology, or physical science majors. B3 lab may be satisfied by CHEM 104L.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Chemistry in Action
courses.description: Chemistry and technological advances; impacts on society and environment. Survey of modern topics.

restrictions.text: B3 lab may be satisfied by CHEM 110L.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: The Physical Environment
courses.description: Nature, distribution and relationships of climate, landforms, vegetation, hydrology and soils.

restrictions.text: Enroll in GEOG 101 only if meeting B3 lab in Life Science.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: The Physical Environment
courses.description: Natural environment including weather, climate, landforms, vegetation, hydrology and soils; landscape change over time.

restrictions.text: No credit for both GEOG 101A and GEOG 101.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Weather
courses.description: Study of atmospheric processes.

restrictions.text: Enroll in GEOG 103 only if meeting B3 lab in Life Science.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Weather
courses.description: Dynamics of Earth's atmosphere; temperature variations, clouds, moisture, wind systems, air masses/fronts; thunderstorms, tornadoes, hurricanes; forecasting.

restrictions.text: No credit for both GEOG 103A and GEOG 103.

courses.subject: Astronomy
courses.title: Observational Astronomy Lab
courses.description: Techniques of observational astronomy including data acquisition and interpretation; testing hypotheses using observations of the Moon, planets, Sun, stars and galaxies.

courses.subject: Anthropology
courses.title: Introduction to Biological Anthropology
courses.description: Biological variation in humans and non-human primates; evolution and behavior in past and present; interactions between biology and culture.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Introductory Biology
courses.description: Major biological concepts for non-science majors: ecology, evolution, heredity, organismal and cellular structure and physiology.

restrictions.text: Not for credit in the Biology major. May not be taken for credit by students who have completed BIOL 101, BIOL 102, BIOL 106 or BIOL 107. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 100L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: General Biology
courses.description: Selected topics illustrating major concepts in biology: evolution, environmental relationships, heredity, the cell, energetics and development.

restrictions.text: Not for credit in the Biology major. May not be taken by students who have completed BIOL 106 or BIOL 107. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 101L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Biological Principles I
courses.description: First course of a two-semester sequence for Biology majors. Scientific process, heredity, evolution, taxonomy/systematics, ecology and animal behavior.

restrictions.text: Primarily for Biology majors. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 106L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Biological Principles II
courses.description: Second course of the sequence for Biology majors. Chemistry of life, cells, molecular genetics, development, plant and animal physiology.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite or preparatory: CHEM 102 and CHEM 102L. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 107L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Microbes and People
courses.description: Microbial world and its involvement in disease, food production and the environment for non-science majors.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite: completion of the lower-division writing requirement. Not available for Biology majors. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 218L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Wild SoCal
courses.description: For non-science majors: field-based study of important wild plants and animals of Southern California; application of scientific methods via case studies.

restrictions.text: Students may not receive credit for both BIOL 292/L and BIOL 323/392E. B3 lab satisfied by BIOL 292L.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: General Physics I Lab
courses.description: Laboratory exercises in mechanics, heat and sound. Fulfills the B3 Science Laboratory Activity requirement when taken with PHYS 100A.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: General Physics II Lab
courses.description: Experiments in electricity, magnetism, light and modern physics. Fulfills the B3 Science Laboratory Activity requirement when taken with PHYS 100B.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Mechanics Lab
courses.description: Experiments in dynamics, statics, vibrations, and fluid mechanics. Fulfills the B3 Science Laboratory Activity requirement when taken with PHYS 220A or PHYS 225.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Electricity and Magnetism Lab
courses.description: Experiments in electric and magnetic fields, circuits, and electromagnetic induction. Fulfills the B3 Science Laboratory Activity requirement when taken with PHYS 220B or PHYS 226.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Principles of Chemistry Lab
courses.description: Laboratory course accompanying CHEM 100 introducing fundamentals of scientific inquiry and basic lab techniques.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: General Chemistry I Lab
courses.description: Laboratory emphasizing basic skills, quantitative relationships, and inorganic preparative techniques. 3 hours per week.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: General Chemistry II Lab
courses.description: Experiments in kinetics, equilibria, reactions of metals and nonmetals, and qualitative analysis.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Introductory Chemistry I Lab
courses.description: Experiments on inorganic chemistry fundamentals: atoms, molecules, periodic table, stoichiometry, and states of matter.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Introductory Chemistry II Lab
courses.description: Continuation of CHEM 103L; experiments on equilibria, acids/bases, and organic chemistry of common elements.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Chemistry in Action Lab
courses.description: Lab-demonstration course accompanying CHEM 100 or CHEM 110; introduces basic lab skills and demonstrations of chemical principles.

courses.subject: Science
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change Lab
courses.description: Laboratory exercises on climate prediction modeling, proxy data, and climate change impacts.

courses.subject: Sustainability
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change Lab
courses.description: Climate prediction modeling, proxy data use, and analysis of climate change effects; shared lab with SCI 111L and GEOG 111L.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: The Physical Environment Lab
courses.description: Lab analyzing climate, landforms, vegetation, hydrology, and soils; supports GEOG 101A.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Weather Lab
courses.description: Lab on dynamics of Earth's atmosphere; temperature, clouds, precipitation, wind, and forecasting.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geology of Planet Earth Lab
courses.description: Field and lab identification of rocks and minerals; interpretation of geologic maps and plate tectonics.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geology Goes Hollywood Lab
courses.description: Online lab exploring Earth science issues as portrayed in film/TV and evaluating accuracy in scientific depictions.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Geological Disasters Lab
courses.description: Lab accompanying GEOL 117; explores geologic hazards such as earthquakes, volcanoes, and floods.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: World Ocean Lab
courses.description: Lab and field study of oceanographic data collection and interpretation accompanying GEOL 122.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Support Course for GE Mathematics — Business
courses.description: Credit/No Credit pre-baccalaureate support for the Business Math course (MATH 103); just-in-time remediation aligned to lecture meetings. May be repeated once for credit.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Support Course for GE Mathematics — STEM
courses.description: Credit/No Credit support for the Calculus sequence; just-in-time remediation aligned to MATH 102 lecture meetings. May be repeated once for credit.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Support Course for GE Mathematics — Statistics
courses.description: Credit/No Credit support for Introductory Statistics aligned to MATH 141 lecture meetings. May be repeated once for credit.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Pre-Calculus I Lab
courses.description: Credit/No Credit hybrid enrichment laboratory aligned to MATH 102; reinforces problem solving and lecture concepts.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Introductory Statistics
courses.description: Introduction to statistical reasoning and methods.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Introductory Statistics Lab
courses.description: Laboratory activities to reinforce MATH 141 concepts. Credit/No Credit.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Pre-Calculus I
courses.description: Preparation for algebra necessary for calculus. Topics include linear, quadratic, and rational expressions, with emphasis on local and global behavior of functions. Intended for computer science, engineering, mathematics, and natural science majors.

restrictions.text: Students conditionally prepared must have credit for or concurrently enroll in MATH 102L. Not open to students with credit in MATH 105.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Mathematical Methods for Business
courses.description: Concepts and applications of algebra and calculus to business, including functions, systems of equations, matrices, and the derivative. Topics emphasize quantitative reasoning in business contexts.

restrictions.text: Students conditionally prepared must have credit for or concurrently enroll in MATH 103L.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Pre-Calculus II
courses.description: Preparation for trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions used in calculus. Builds proficiency in analyzing the behavior of exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric expressions.

restrictions.text: Students conditionally prepared must have credit for or concurrently enroll in MATH 105L.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Mathematical Foundations for Non-Calculus Physics
courses.description: Mathematics applicable to non-calculus physics: inequalities, functions and graphs, vectors, angular velocity, and parametric equations. Emphasis on applied algebra and trigonometry.

restrictions.text: Not open to students with credit in MATH 105 or MATH 255A.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Mathematical Ideas
courses.description: Exploration of mathematical ideas and reasoning for non-majors. Introduces logic, patterns, and problem-solving approaches used in mathematics.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite: Multiple Measures Placement in GE-level Mathematics or credit in MATH 093, MATH 196QR, or MATH 196S (or equivalent).

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Introductory Statistics
courses.description: Methods for displaying, describing, and producing data; normal distribution, correlation, regression, sampling distributions, and probability. Emphasis on statistical inference for means and proportions.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite: Multiple Measures Placement in GE-level Mathematics or credit in MATH 093, MATH 196QR, or MATH 196S (or equivalent).

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Calculus I
courses.description: Differential and integral calculus of one variable. Topics include limits, derivatives, applications of differentiation, and the fundamentals of integration.

restrictions.text: Students conditionally prepared or transferring credit for MATH 105 or both MATH 102 and MATH 104 must have credit for or concurrently enroll in MATH 150AL.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Calculus for the Life Sciences I
courses.description: Introduction to calculus for life science applications. Includes limits, differentiation, optimization, and integration with an emphasis on biological and health contexts.

restrictions.text: Students conditionally prepared must have credit for or concurrently enroll in MATH 255AL. Not open to students with credit in MATH 150A. Knowledge of trigonometry is assumed.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Field Studies in Biology — Wild SoCal Lab
courses.description: Field-based lab to accompany BIOL 292. Emphasizes scientific observation, data collection and interpretation in SoCal habitats.

restrictions.text: 3 hours per week or equivalent; course fee. B2 Life Science satisfied by completing BIOL 292.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Problem Solving in General Chemistry I
courses.description: Structured group work developing analysis and problem-solving skills for CHEM 101 topics.

courses.subject: Chemistry
courses.title: Problem Solving in General Chemistry II
courses.description: Problem-solving discussion supporting CHEM 102; worked examples and individual problems.

restrictions.text: Prerequisite grade of C- or better in CHEM 101.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Biological Principles II Lab
courses.description: Laboratory in biological chemistry, cells, molecular genetics, development, and physiology; observations, experiments, demonstrations.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite/preparatory: CHEM 102 and CHEM 102L. B2 satisfied by completing BIOL 107.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change
courses.description: Scientific basis of climate change; causes, impacts, projections, and mitigation within social/economic contexts.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one: GEOG 111, SUST 111, or SCI 111. B3 lab may be satisfied by GEOG 111L.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Understanding Climate Change Lab
courses.description: Lab on climate prediction modeling, proxy data, and analysis of climate-change impacts.

restrictions.text: Credit for only one lab: GEOG 111L, SUST 111L, or SCI 111L.

courses.subject: Geography
courses.title: Climate Change Lab
courses.description: Laboratory to accompany GEOG 112 on climate science across spatial and temporal scales; sustainability context.

restrictions.text: No credit in addition to GEOG 111L, SUST 111L, or SCI 111L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Biological Principles I Lab
courses.description: Lab for Biology majors covering scientific process, heredity, evolution, taxonomy/systematics, ecology, and animal behavior.

restrictions.text: B2 Biological Science satisfied by completing BIOL 106.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Introductory Biology Lab
courses.description: Observations, experiments, demonstrations, and required field trips to augment BIOL 100; emphasis on methods of science and biological principles.

restrictions.text: Not for credit in the Biology major. No credit if completed BIOL 101L, BIOL 102LCS, BIOL 106L, or BIOL 107L.

courses.subject: Biology
courses.title: Microbes and People Lab
courses.description: Lab for non-science majors on microbes in disease, food production, and the environment; demonstrations and experiments.

restrictions.text: Not available for Biology majors. B2 satisfied by completing BIOL 218.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Calculus II
courses.description: Techniques of integration and improper integrals; sequences and series; power series and Taylor polynomials; parametric and polar coordinates; vectors and solid geometry.

courses.subject: Mathematics
courses.title: Calculus for the Life Sciences II
courses.description: Second semester of the brief life-sciences calculus sequence. Techniques of integration, introduction to differential equations, probability applications, elements of multivariable calculus, and linear algebra with life-science applications.

restrictions.text: Not open to students who have successfully completed MATH 150AB.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Physics I (Calculus-based)
courses.description: Calculus-based mechanics for physical science majors: kinematics and dynamics, fluids, waves, and acoustics.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite or preparatory: MATH 150B.

courses.subject: Physics
courses.title: Physics II (Calculus-based)
courses.description: Continuation for physical science majors: electricity and magnetism, circuits, optics, and related topics.

restrictions.text: Recommended corequisite or preparatory: MATH 250.

courses.subject: Geology
courses.title: Earth and Life Through Time Lab
courses.description: Laboratory accompanying GEOL 113. Introduces fossils, relative-age relationships, and construction and interpretation of maps and cross-sections emphasizing paleogeography and sedimentary rocks. Includes one-day field trip.

restrictions.text: Available for General Education, B3 Science Laboratory Activity provided GEOL 113 is also completed.

areas.name: A. English Language Communication & Critical Thinking
F. Comparative Cultural Studies / GRC&E / Foreign Languages
